title: salvia nemorosa subsp. valentina
intro: Salvia nemorosa subsp. valentina, also known as woodland sage, is a subspecies of the Salvia nemorosa plant, a perennial herb native to Europe. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional herbal medicine, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Salvia nemorosa subsp. valentina include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and soothe respiratory issues such as coughs and colds, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is often used in conjunction with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
